title: When Job Protection Backfires
subtitle: 保护就业为何反而拖累绩效？新西兰的教训

paragraphs.en: The personal grievance provisions of New Zealand's Employment Relations Act 2000 prevent an employer from firing an employee without good cause. Instead, dismissals must be justified. Employers must both show cause and act in a procedurally fair way. Personal grievance procedures were designed to guard the jobs of ordinary workers from "unjustified dismissals."
paragraphs.zh: 新西兰2000年《雇佣关系法》中的个人申诉条款规定，雇主不得无故解雇员工。相反，解雇必须有正当理由。雇主既要证明解雇事由成立，也须在程序上做到公平公正。个人申诉程序的设计初衷，是为了保护普通劳动者的工作免受“不合理解雇”的侵害。

paragraphs.en: The premise was that the common law of contract lacked sufficient safeguards for workers against arbitrary conduct by management. Long gone are the days when a boss could simply give an employee contractual notice. But these provisions create difficulties for businesses when applied to highly paid managers and executives.
paragraphs.zh: 这一制度的前提是，普通合同法对劳动者的保护尚不足以抵御管理层的专断行为。老板仅需按合同规定提前通知即可解雇员工的日子早已一去不返。然而，当这些条款适用于高薪经理和高管时，却给企业带来了重重困难。

paragraphs.en: As countless boards and business owners will attest, constraining firms from firing poorly performing, high-earning managers is a handbrake on boosting productivity and overall performance. The difference between C-grade and A-grade managers may very well be the difference between business success or failure. Between preserving the jobs of ordinary workers or losing them.
paragraphs.zh: 正如无数董事会和企业主所证实的那样，限制企业解雇业绩不佳的高薪管理者，无异于给提升生产力和整体绩效拉起了手刹。C级经理与A级经理之间的差距，很可能就决定了企业的成败，也决定了普通员工的饭碗是保住还是丢掉。

paragraphs.en: Yet mediocrity is no longer enough to justify a dismissal. Consequently — and paradoxically — laws introduced to protect the jobs of ordinary workers may be placing those jobs at risk. If not placing jobs at risk, to the extent employment protection laws constrain business owners from dismissing under-performing managers, those laws act as a constraint on firm productivity and therefore on workers' wages.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，表现平庸已不再构成解雇的充分理由。于是便出现了一个矛盾的结果：那些旨在保护普通劳动者饭碗的法律，反而可能将这些工作置于险境。即便工作本身暂无风险，只要就业保护法限制了企业主解雇表现欠佳的管理者，这些法律就会成为制约企业生产力、进而抑制工人工资增长的枷锁。

paragraphs.en: Indeed, in an international perspective on New Zealand's productivity paradox, the Productivity Commission singled out the low quality of managerial capabilities as a cause of the country's poor productivity growth record. Nor are highly paid managers themselves immune from the harm caused by the unjustified dismissal procedures.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，在一份关于新西兰生产力悖论的国际视角报告中，生产力委员会明确指出，管理能力的低劣是该国生产力增长记录不佳的原因之一。而高薪管理者自身也未能免受不合理解雇程序带来的伤害。

words.meaning: adj. 免疫的；不受影响的

paragraphs.en: Because employment protection laws make it costlier to fire an employee, employers are more cautious about hiring new staff. This makes it harder for the marginal manager to gain employment. And firms pay staff less because firms carry the burden of the employment arrangement going wrong.
paragraphs.zh: 由于就业保护法提高了裁员成本，雇主在招聘新员工时便更加谨慎。这使得边缘管理者更难找到工作。同时，企业支付给员工的工资也相应降低，因为企业承担了雇佣关系出错的额外负担。

paragraphs.en: Society also suffers from excessive employment protections. Stringent job dismissal regulations adversely affect productivity growth and hamper both prosperity and overall well-being. Across the Tasman, Australia deals with the unjustified dismissal paradox by excluding employees earning above a specified high-income threshold from the protection of its unfair dismissal laws.
paragraphs.zh: 社会同样因过度的就业保护而蒙受损失。严格的解雇法规对生产率增长产生了负面影响，阻碍了繁荣与整体福祉的提升。在塔斯曼海对岸，澳大利亚处理不合理解雇悖论的方法是，将收入超过特定“高薪门槛”的员工排除在不公平解雇法的保护之外。

paragraphs.en: In New Zealand, a 2016 private members' Bill tried to permit firms and high-income employees to contract out of the unjustified dismissal regime. However, the mechanisms proposed were unwieldy and the Bill was voted down following the change in government later that year.
paragraphs.zh: 在新西兰，2016年一项议员个人提案曾试图允许企业和高收入雇员通过合同约定选择不适用不合理解雇制度。然而，提案中的机制过于繁琐，且在该年晚些时候政府更迭后，该法案遭到否决。
